name: ecec
tagline: Precision Timing Solutions for Modern Electronics
description: ECEC is a leading manufacturer of quartz crystal resonators, crystal oscillators, and timing components for consumer electronics, telecommunications, automotive, and industrial applications. As an authorized ECEC distributor, we provide comprehensive product selection and technical support.
longDescription: ECEC (Dongjing Electronics) is a premier Chinese manufacturer specializing in frequency control and timing solutions. Founded in 1999 and headquartered in Taizhou, Zhejiang, ECEC has established itself as a trusted supplier of high-quality quartz crystal resonators, crystal oscillators, and MEMS timing devices. The company operates state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities with advanced crystal growing, precision machining, and automated testing capabilities. ECEC's product portfolio spans HC-49/S, SMD crystal resonators, temperature-compensated crystal oscillators (TCXO), voltage-controlled crystal oscillators (VCXO), and oven-controlled crystal oscillators (OCXO). With certifications including ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and IATF 16949, ECEC serves global markets across consumer electronics, telecommunications, automotive, IoT, and industrial sectors. As your authorized ECEC distributor, we offer expert product selection guidance, competitive pricing, and reliable supply chain support.

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of ECEC timing components?
faqs.answer: ECEC timing components offer several key advantages: 1) Cost-effectiveness - competitive pricing compared to international brands while maintaining quality. 2) Wide frequency range - covering 32.768kHz to 200MHz for diverse applications. 3) Package variety - HC-49/S, SMD 2.0x1.6mm to 7.0x5.0mm, and specialized packages. 4) High reliability - rigorous testing ensures long-term stability. 5) Local support - responsive technical support and fast delivery. 6) Customization - ability to provide custom frequencies and specifications. 7) Automotive qualification - AEC-Q200 qualified products for automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our ECEC timing component catalog to find the right product for your application.

faqs.question: How do I select the right crystal resonator for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right crystal resonator involves several key factors: 1) Frequency - match your system clock requirement exactly. 2) Package - choose based on PCB space (SMD for compact, HC-49/S for through-hole). 3) Frequency tolerance - ±10ppm to ±50ppm depending on accuracy needs. 4) Load capacitance - match your oscillator circuit design (typically 8pF to 20pF). 5) Operating temperature - commercial (0 to +70C) or industrial (-40 to +85C). 6) Drive level - ensure sufficient power for reliable oscillation. 7) Aging - consider long-term frequency stability requirements. Our FAE team can help you select the optimal ECEC crystal for your specific application.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your frequency, accuracy, and package requirements for personalized crystal selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between crystal resonators and crystal oscillators?
faqs.answer: Crystal resonators are passive components that require an external oscillator circuit to generate a clock signal. They offer lower cost and lower power consumption but require careful circuit design. Crystal oscillators (XO) are complete active modules containing the crystal and oscillator circuit in one package. They provide guaranteed startup, better frequency stability, and easier integration but at higher cost and power consumption. Types include: Standard XO - basic clock generation. TCXO - temperature-compensated for high stability. VCXO - voltage-controlled for frequency adjustment. OCXO - oven-controlled for highest stability. Choose resonators for cost-sensitive designs with available oscillator circuits. Choose oscillators for guaranteed performance and simplified design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use crystal resonators for cost optimization with external oscillator circuits. Use crystal oscillators for guaranteed startup and simplified design.

faqs.question: Does ECEC offer automotive-qualified products?
faqs.answer: Yes, ECEC offers AEC-Q200 qualified crystal resonators and oscillators for automotive applications. These products undergo rigorous qualification testing including temperature cycling, mechanical shock, vibration, and high-temperature storage. Automotive-grade products feature: Extended temperature range -40C to +125C. Enhanced reliability screening. Tighter frequency tolerance options. Comprehensive qualification documentation. They are used in infotainment systems, instrument clusters, body control modules, ADAS, and telematics applications. The AEC-Q200 qualification ensures reliable operation in harsh automotive environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our automotive-qualified ECEC products or contact us for automotive component recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I calculate load capacitance for crystal resonators?
faqs.answer: Load capacitance (CL) is critical for crystal oscillator circuit design. The total load capacitance seen by the crystal is calculated as: CL = (C1 * C2) / (C1 + C2) + Cstray. Where C1 and C2 are the external load capacitors, and Cstray is the stray capacitance from PCB traces and IC pins (typically 3-5pF). For a crystal requiring 12pF load capacitance: If Cstray = 4pF, then (C1 * C2) / (C1 + C2) = 12 - 4 = 8pF. With C1 = C2, each capacitor should be 16pF. Common practice is to use equal values for C1 and C2. Standard values are 8pF, 12pF, 16pF, 20pF. Always verify actual oscillation frequency and adjust if necessary. Use NP0/C0G ceramic capacitors for temperature stability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for load capacitance calculation assistance and circuit design review.
